courseleaf.title: Environmental Horticulture, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
Related Undergraduate Programs

See the undergraduate majors in Plant Biology & Plant Sciences.

Related Courses

See Plant Biology (PLB) & Plant Sciences (PLS).

Minor Advisor

Plant Sciences Advising; Advising
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Successful completion of the minor requires the following:

  • All courses must be taken for a letter grade.
  • Minimum overall GPA of 2.000.
  • No more than one course can be counted towards both the student’s major and the minor.
Course List
Code Title Units
ENH 006Introduction to Environmental Plants4
ENH 105 (Discontinued for fall 2025) **4
PLS 171Principles & Practices of Plant Propagation4
Choose three:11-13
ENH 100
Urban Forests are Nature-Based Solutions
ENH 120
Management of Container Media
ENH 125
Greenhouse & Protected Cultivation Systems
ENH 133
Woody Plants in the Landscape: Growth, Ecology & Management
Total Units23-25
**

Course(s) discontinued; see your advisor for course options.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Are you interested in the design of urban landscapes as well as the plants used for intensive and/or urban agriculture? The Environmental Horticulture minor broadens your understanding of the science behind the identification, propagation, and maintenance of plants for human use, whether in a landscape or production setting.
